channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Alkaline cement tiles boost baby coral survival from 12% to 52% - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Alkaline cement tiles boost baby coral survival from 12% to 52%  Phys.org
  2. Alkalinity-enhanced artificial substrates modulate local pH and increase survivorship of early-stage coral recruits  Nature

item.title: SpaceX wins its first MARS contract but it comes with a catch - Teslarati
item.description:
1. SpaceX wins its first MARS contract but it comes with a catch  Teslarati
  2. NASA Begins Implementation for ESA’s Rosalind Franklin Mission to Mars  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. NASA selects Falcon Heavy to launch ESA Mars rover mission despite budget threat  SpaceNews
  4. After a saga of broken promises, a European rover finally has a ride to Mars  Ars Technica
  5. SpaceX Won A Mars Mission That Might Get Cancelled  Payload Space

item.title: What Physical ‘Life Force’ Turns Biology’s Wheels? - Quanta Magazine
item.description: What Physical ‘Life Force’ Turns Biology’s Wheels?  Quanta Magazine

item.title: See ‘Shooting Stars’ And A Lingering Comet Pan-STARRS On Tuesday - Forbes
item.description:
1. See ‘Shooting Stars’ And A Lingering Comet Pan-STARRS On Tuesday  Forbes
  2. Comet Tracker: Monday Brings Best Chance To See A Comet And Meteors  Yahoo
  3. This is the comet everyone wants to see at the moment. One photographer captured it shedding its tail  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  4. The Sky Today on Tuesday, April 21: Could this be your last glimpse of Comet PanSTARRS?  Astronomy Magazine
  5. Comet C/2025 R3 (PANSTARRS) tail blowing in the Solar Wind  Sky & Telescope

item.title: New Triassic Dinosaur Species Identified in New Mexico - Sci.News
item.description:
1. New Triassic Dinosaur Species Identified in New Mexico  Sci.News
  2. Student identifies new meat-eating dinosaur three times older than T. rex  Virginia Tech News
  3. How an undergraduate student discovered a new dinosaur, nicknamed ‘murder muppet’  WVTF
  4. Crushed skull fossil rewrites the final chapter of the earliest dinosaurs  Earth.com
  5. A crushed fossil revealed a dinosaur that shouldn’t have existed  Science Daily

item.title: What Have We Dumped On The Moon? - Hackaday
item.description: What Have We Dumped On The Moon?  Hackaday

item.title: Why ‘little red dots’ pose a big mystery about the Universe - Aeon
item.description:
1. Why ‘little red dots’ pose a big mystery about the Universe  Aeon
  2. Astronomers have found 340 tiny red dots that shouldn’t exist in the early universe, and they may be just the beginning  ecoportal.net
  3. Saturday citations: Neuroinflammaging treatment stuns; a hidden magma lake; decoding little red dots  MSN
  4. Little red dots: New clues from the early universe  EurekAlert!
  5. Webb's Little Red Dots may reveal how giant black holes formed soon after the Big Bang  Phys.org

item.title: Could the mathematical 'shape' of the universe solve the cosmological constant problem? - Phys.org
item.description: Could the mathematical 'shape' of the universe solve the cosmological constant problem?  Phys.org
